original: "Star WarsEpisode V - Empire Strikes Back", produced byGeorge Lucasopens in cinemas in UK and North America
Stanislav Petrov receives the World Citizen Award for averting a potential nuclear war in 1983, after he correctly identified a fault in the Soviet early warning system
After flying for 17 hours from Newfoundland,Amelia Earhartlands near Londonderry, Northern Ireland, completing the first solo transatlantic flight by a woman
AviatorCharles Lindbergh, in the Spirit of St. Louis, lands in Paris after the first solo air crossing of the Atlantic
Barnum & BaileyCircus performs for the last time at the Nassau Coliseum in NYC after 146 years
BTSwins the Top Social Artist at the Billboard Awards, becoming the first K-pop group to win any Billboard Award
Mary Anning, British fossil collector, dealer, and palaeontologist (Jurassic marine fossil beds of Lyme Regis), born in Lyme Regis, Dorset (d. 1847)
Michelangelo's Pietà in St. Peter's Basilica in Rome is damaged by a vandal
US House of Representatives passes amendment allowing women to vote
American Red Cross founded byClara Barton
Leontyne Pricebecomes the first African American to sing the lead at Teatro alla Scala in Milan in "Aida"
Reggae musicianBob Marleyreceives a Jamaican state funeral
Marvin Gaye' eleventh album "What's Going On" is released; considered the best album off all-time by critic surveys in Rolling Stone (2020); The Guardian (1997); and New Music Express (1985)
Microsoft announces the release of Xbox One
Challenger moves to Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida), for mating for STS-7 mission
1st nuclear-powered lighthouse begins operations (Chesapeake Bay)
Hans Berger, German neuroscientist, psychiatrist, and inventor of electroencephalography (EEG), born in Neuses, German Empire (d. 1941)
John Alexander Hopps, Canadian scientist (co-developer of the first artificial pace-maker), born in Winnipeg, Manitoba (d. 1998)[1]
Glenn Hammond Curtiss, American inventor (hydroplane) and founder of the US aviation industry, born in Hammondsport, New York (d. 1930)
Willem Einthoven, Dutch physiologist and inventor of the electrocardiodiogram (Nobel Prize in Medicine 1924), born in Semarang, Dutch East Indies (d. 1927)
Muhammad AliTKOs Henry Cooper in 6 for heavyweight boxing title
San Diego Padres beat Mont Expos, 11-8, in 21 innings
NY YankJoe Dimaggiohits for cycle (single, double, triple, HR)
Stevie Wonder's single "Sir Duke", a tribute toDuke Ellingtonand other musical influences of Wonder's, begins a 3-week run at #1
Monty Stratton, American baseball pitcher (MLB All Star 1937 Chicago WS; played minor leagues with prosthetic leg; 1949 film The Stratton Story), born in Palacios, Texas (d. 1982)
US Coast Guard Ensign Jean Marie Butler is 1st woman to graduate from US service academy

On same card, American boxer Jermell Charlo KOs John Jackson in 8th to claim vacant WBC super welterweight title, and Jermall Charlo beats Austin Trout on points to retain IBF version; first twins to hold world championships in same weight division
Cleveland Metroparks Zoo Children's Petting Farm opens (Cleveland, Ohio)
Last episode of "Newhart" airs on CBS-TV, with a special twist of an ending
Carolyn Lawrence, American actress and voice actress (SpongeBob SquarePants), born in Baltimore, Maryland
Raymond Burr, Canadian-American actor (Perry Mason; Ironsides; Godzilla), born in New Westminster, British Columbia, Canada (d. 1993)
First American horror movie silent film "Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde" premieres in Chicago
1st bicycles (swift walkers) in US introduced in NYC
Norwegian explorer Roald Amundsun leaves Spitsbergen with 2 seaplanes for the North Pole
Battle of Aspern-Essling: Austrian Archduke Charles beatsNapoleonand his army. Napoleon's first defeat in 10 years.
Napoleonand his forces abandon their siege of Acre after two months - turning point in French invasion of Egypt and Syria
Earthquake centered on Dover Straits with estimated magnitude of 6.0 causes widespread damage, including to Canterbury Cathedral
Federation Internationale de Football Association (FIFA) forms in Paris
Albrecht Dürer, German Renaissance painter and printmaker (Adam and Eve), born in Nuremberg, Germany (d. 1528)
Henri Rousseau, French post-impressionist painter (The Dream, The Hungry Lion Throws Itself on the Antelope), born in Laval, France (d. 1910)
Yerkes Observatory 40" (1m) refractor used for 1st time
Atlanta Brave Rafael Ramirez hits 4 doubles in a game
The Great Fire of Atlanta: at least 10,000 people were displaced, but there was only one fatality
Stanley Cup Final, Nassau Coliseum, Uniondale, NY: NY Islanders win back-to-back titles; beat Minnesota North Stars, 5-1 for 4 games to 1 series win
Stanley Cup Final, Montreal Forum, Montreal, Quebec: 4 consecutive titles for Montreal Canadiens; beat NY Rangers, 4-1 for a 4 games to 1 series win
Mel Stottlemyreties MLB record by walking 11, but wins 2-0 over the Washington Senators at Yankee Stadium
Fastest 9 inning AL baseball night game (89 mins), Chicago White Sox beat visiting Washington Senators, 1-0
Billy Williams of the Chicago Cubs sets outfielder record of 695 straight MLB games
Australian boxer Peter Jackson and future world heavyweight champion Jim Corbett fight a No Contest in 61 rounds at California Athletic Club, San Francisco
Swedish ice hockey team Tre Kronor takes gold in the World Championship, first nation to hold both the World and Olympic titles in the same year
Australian Services win 1st Victory Test Cricket at Lord's by 6 wickets
Rollin Kirby's "On the Road to Moscow" is the 1st cartoon to receive a Pulitzer Prize
David Bowie's "Let's Dance" single goes #1
Electric Light Orchestra releases "Telephone Line" in the UK; it would peak in the Top 10 in the UK and US
"Da'Butt" by EU hits #35
Paul McCartneyand girlfriend Jane Asher attend an Andy Williams concert

The opening of the Ninth Street Show, otherwise known as the 9th Street Art Exhibition - a gathering of a number of notable artists, and the stepping-out of the post war New York avant-garde, collectively know as the New York School.
1st steamship arrives in Hawaii
Portuguese explorer João Da Nova discovers the uninhabited Saint Helena island in the South Atlantic Ocean
Mary Campbell is abducted from her home in Pennsylvania by Lenape during the French and Indian War
